explicit_text: I am a Karen, which is a minority ethnic group from Burma. The Karen people have faced many challenges, especially with the conflict in Burma, and many of us have had to flee for safety. In Minnesota, there is the largest population of Karen refugees, about 12,000, and it feels like home for many of us. I have been involved in serving the Karen community, including helping establish Karen Human Services of America to provide culturally appropriate support.
implicit_text: I am part of an ethnic minority group from Burma that has faced many challenges, especially due to the conflict there, forcing many of us to flee for safety. In Minnesota, there is the largest population of refugees from my group, about 12,000, and it feels like home for many of us. I have been involved in serving this community, including helping establish a human services organization to provide culturally appropriate support.

explicit_text: I was born and raised in Burma, where my life was very difficult because of my father's role as a peacemaker among ethnic minorities. The Burmese military considered my father one of the most wanted people, and because of that, my life was in danger. I had to live secretly with a different name and family to avoid capture or torture. Eventually, I escaped from Burma to Thailand to seek refugee status and safety.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in the country formerly known as Burma, where my life was very difficult because of my father's role as a peacemaker among ethnic minorities. The military considered my father one of the most wanted people, and because of that, my life was in danger. I had to live secretly with a different name and family to avoid capture or torture. Eventually, I escaped from that country to Thailand to seek refugee status and safety.

explicit_text: I chose to resettle in Minnesota because it has the largest Karen refugee population in the United States, about 12,000 people. Minnesota feels like home to us because we can find our culture, food, and the resources we need to adjust to life here. I have been working in Ramsey County, Minnesota, as a social worker serving Karen youth and refugees, helping them with medical and mental health needs.
implicit_text: I chose to resettle in the state known for having the largest refugee population from my ethnic group in the United States, about 12,000 people. This state feels like home to us because we can find our culture, food, and the resources we need to adjust to life here. I have been working in a county within this state as a social worker serving youth and refugees from my community, helping them with medical and mental health needs.

explicit_text: After escaping from Burma, I fled to Thailand where I stayed near the Thai-Burma border. It was a frightening time hiding from soldiers, but I was lucky to be rescued and escorted safely by men sent by my father. In Thailand, I met Dr. Cynthia Maung, a Karen doctor who provides free healthcare to refugees, and I volunteered as a midwife, nurse educator, and counselor at her clinic.
implicit_text: After escaping from my home country, I fled to the neighboring country of Thailand where I stayed near the border with Burma. It was a frightening time hiding from soldiers, but I was lucky to be rescued and escorted safely by men sent by my father. In Thailand, I met a doctor from my ethnic group who provides free healthcare to refugees, and I volunteered as a midwife, nurse educator, and counselor at her clinic.

explicit_text: Dr. Cynthia Maung is a Karen doctor I met in Thailand who provides free healthcare to immigrant refugees along the Thai-Burma border. She became a mentor to me as I volunteered at her clinic as a midwife, nurse educator, and counselor. Her work inspired me during my time in Thailand before I resettled in the United States.
implicit_text: I met a doctor from my ethnic group in Thailand who provides free healthcare to immigrant refugees along the border with Burma. She became a mentor to me as I volunteered at her clinic as a midwife, nurse educator, and counselor. Her work inspired me during my time in Thailand before I resettled in the United States.

explicit_text: I came to the United States in 2009 as a refugee seeking safety and a new life after escaping Burma and Thailand. I chose the U.S. because it accepts many refugees from Burma, and I wanted to continue my education here. Since arriving, I have been studying clinical social work and working to support the Karen community through social services and by helping establish Karen Human Services of America.
implicit_text: I came to this country in 2009 as a refugee seeking safety and a new life after escaping Burma and Thailand. I chose this nation because it accepts many refugees from Burma, and I wanted to continue my education here. Since arriving, I have been studying clinical social work and working to support my ethnic community through social services and by helping establish a human services organization.

explicit_text: I am currently pursuing a licensed clinical social work degree at Metropolitan State University. This education is important to me as I want to better serve the Karen community and address their medical and mental health needs. Studying here has helped me become more engaged and independent in American society.
implicit_text: I am currently pursuing a licensed clinical social work degree at a state university in the Midwest. This education is important to me as I want to better serve my ethnic community and address their medical and mental health needs. Studying here has helped me become more engaged and independent in American society.

explicit_text: I work as a social worker in Ramsey County, Minnesota, where I serve Karen youth and refugees. Many in the Karen community here struggle with accessing culturally appropriate medical and mental health services, so my role is vital. Ramsey County has become a place where I can help my people adjust and thrive in this new environment.
implicit_text: I work as a social worker in a county in Minnesota, where I serve youth and refugees from my ethnic group. Many in this community here struggle with accessing culturally appropriate medical and mental health services, so my role is vital. This county has become a place where I can help my people adjust and thrive in this new environment.

explicit_text: In 2014, I helped establish Karen Human Services of America, a 501c3 non-profit organization dedicated to serving the Karen community in the U.S. I have seven board members who share the mission to provide culturally appropriate services that are currently lacking. This organization is part of my legacy to carry on my father's peacemaking work and to give back to my community.
